colorize.h(3) Library Functions Manual colorize.h(3)

NAME

colorize.h - Log message colorization for terminal output.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


const char * colorize_log_message (const char *message)
Colorize a log message for terminal output.

Detailed Description

Log message colorization for terminal output.

Colorizes log messages for terminal output by applying colors to:

  • Numbers and numeric units (cyan/light blue)
  • File paths like /path/to/file or C:\path (magenta)
  • Environment variables like $VAR_NAME (grey)

Only applies colors when output goes to TTY (not when piped). File logging output remains uncolorized.

Definition in file colorize.h.

Function Documentation

const char * colorize_log_message (const char * message)

Colorize a log message for terminal output. Applies semantic colors to:

  • Numeric values: 0, 25, 0.24, 1920x1080, 25 MB, 1024 GiB, etc. (cyan)
  • File paths: /path/to/file, C:\path, src/main.c, etc. (magenta)
  • Environment variables: $VAR_NAME, $HOME, etc. (grey)

Parameters

message The log message to colorize

Returns

Colorized string in static buffer (reused across calls)

Note

Caller must use returned string immediately before next call

Only colorizes if output is going to TTY, otherwise returns message unchanged

Uses 4 rotating static buffers like colored_string() to handle multiple colorizations in a single expression.

Only applies colors if output is going to a TTY (not piped/redirected). Only colorizes text that is not already colored (in reset state).

Definition at line 512 of file colorize.c.

512                                                       {
513   if (!message) {
514     return message;
515   }
516 
517   // Check if colors should be used based on TTY status (same as ASCII art)
518   // Colors are only applied when output is actually a TTY
519   bool should_colorize = terminal_should_color_output(STDOUT_FILENO);
520 
521   if (!should_colorize) {
522     return message;
523   }
524 
525   // Use 4 static buffers for rotation (handles nested calls)
526   static char buffers[4][4096];
527   static int buffer_index = 0;
528 
529   char *output = buffers[buffer_index];
530   buffer_index = (buffer_index + 1) % 4;
531 
532   size_t out_pos = 0;
533   const size_t max_size = sizeof(buffers[0]);
534 
535   // Process the input string
536   for (size_t i = 0; message[i] != '\0' && out_pos < max_size - 100; i++) {
537     size_t end_pos = 0;
538 
539     // Check if already colorized - only colorize if NOT already colored
540     bool can_colorize = !ansi_is_already_colorized(message, i);
541 
542     // Try key=value pair first (highest priority pattern)
543     size_t key_end = 0, value_start = 0, value_end = 0;
544     if (can_colorize && is_key_value_pair(message, i, &key_end, &value_start, &value_end)) {
545       size_t key_len = key_end - i;
546       size_t value_len = value_end - value_start;
547 
548       // Extract key
549       char key_buf[256];
550       safe_snprintf(key_buf, sizeof(key_buf), "%.*s", (int)key_len, message + i);
551 
552       // Extract value
553       char value_buf[512];
554       safe_snprintf(value_buf, sizeof(value_buf), "%.*s", (int)value_len, message + value_start);
555 
556       // Color the key (magenta)
557       const char *colored_key = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_FATAL, key_buf);
558       size_t colored_key_len = strlen(colored_key);
559 
560       // Check if we have enough space for key + equals + value
561       // (accounting for ANSI codes which will be added by colored_string)
562       if (out_pos + colored_key_len + 1 + value_len + 100 < max_size) {
563         memcpy(output + out_pos, colored_key, colored_key_len);
564         out_pos += colored_key_len;
565 
566         // Add uncolored equals sign
567         output[out_pos++] = '=';
568 
569         // Determine value color and add colored value
570         log_color_t value_color = get_value_color(value_buf);
571         const char *colored_value = colored_string(value_color, value_buf);
572         size_t colored_value_len = strlen(colored_value);
573         if (out_pos + colored_value_len < max_size) {
574           memcpy(output + out_pos, colored_value, colored_value_len);
575           out_pos += colored_value_len;
576         }
577       }
578       i = value_end - 1;
579       continue;
580     }
581 
582     // Try numeric pattern
583     if (can_colorize && is_numeric_pattern(message, i, &end_pos)) {
584       size_t pattern_len = end_pos - i;
585       char pattern_buf[256];
586       safe_snprintf(pattern_buf, sizeof(pattern_buf), "%.*s", (int)pattern_len, message + i);
587 
588       const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, pattern_buf);
589       size_t colored_len = strlen(colored);
590       if (out_pos + colored_len < max_size) {
591         memcpy(output + out_pos, colored, colored_len);
592         out_pos += colored_len;
593       }
594       i = end_pos - 1;
595       continue;
596     }
597 
598     // Try file path
599     if (can_colorize && is_file_path(message, i, &end_pos)) {
600       size_t path_len = end_pos - i;
601       char path_buf[512];
602       safe_snprintf(path_buf, sizeof(path_buf), "%.*s", (int)path_len, message + i);
603 
604       const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, path_buf);
605       size_t colored_len = strlen(colored);
606       if (out_pos + colored_len < max_size) {
607         memcpy(output + out_pos, colored, colored_len);
608         out_pos += colored_len;
609       }
610       i = end_pos - 1;
611       continue;
612     }
613 
614     // Try environment variable
615     if (can_colorize && is_env_var(message, i, &end_pos)) {
616       size_t var_len = end_pos - i;
617       char var_buf[256];
618       safe_snprintf(var_buf, sizeof(var_buf), "%.*s", (int)var_len, message + i);
619 
620       const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_GREY, var_buf);
621       size_t colored_len = strlen(colored);
622       if (out_pos + colored_len < max_size) {
623         memcpy(output + out_pos, colored, colored_len);
624         out_pos += colored_len;
625       }
626       i = end_pos - 1;
627       continue;
628     }
629 
630     // Try URL
631     if (can_colorize && is_url(message, i, &end_pos)) {
632       size_t url_len = end_pos - i;
633       char url_buf[2048];
634       safe_snprintf(url_buf, sizeof(url_buf), "%.*s", (int)url_len, message + i);
635 
636       const char *colored = colored_string(LOG_COLOR_INFO, url_buf);
637       size_t colored_len = strlen(colored);
638       if (out_pos + colored_len < max_size) {
639         memcpy(output + out_pos, colored, colored_len);
640         out_pos += colored_len;
641       }
642       i = end_pos - 1;
643       continue;
644     }
645 
646     // Regular character - just copy
647     if (out_pos < max_size - 1) {
648       output[out_pos++] = message[i];
649     }
650   }
651 
652   output[out_pos] = '\0';
653   return output;
654 }




References ansi_is_already_colorized(),
    colored_string(), LOG_COLOR_DEBUG, LOG_COLOR_FATAL,
    LOG_COLOR_GREY, LOG_COLOR_INFO, safe_snprintf(), and
    terminal_should_color_output().


Referenced by log_msg(), log_plain_msg(),
    log_recolor_plain_entry(), and log_template_apply().
